profile.thesis: Build the R&D command system for a future canonical personal Islamic environment that compounds over years without silent corruption.
profile.horizon: Three-year preparation runway before formal Islamic study becomes central.
profile.canonicalEndState: One evolving personal Islamic hub where knowledge, workflow, memory, and study operations converge.
profile.operatorRole: Owner supplies resources and high-value personal context; Codex leads product strategy and system design.

evidenceLinks.title: Paperclip architecture
evidenceLinks.note: Useful as a multi-agent control plane reference, not yet the canonical backbone.

evidenceLinks.title: OpenAI Agents SDK
evidenceLinks.note: Use for structured handoffs, tools, and agent orchestration inside the control plane.

evidenceLinks.title: Temporal docs
evidenceLinks.note: Durable execution reference considered and deferred in v1 for stack simplicity.

critiqueArtifacts.title: Adversarial Architecture & Integrity Review
critiqueArtifacts.summary: External adversarial review of the Codex-led reset and runtime architecture.
critiqueArtifacts.findingsSummary: 15 findings ordered by severity, including 3 critical and 5 high.

decisions.title: Restore IsIdeas to factory-only scope
decisions.summary: IsIdeas now stops at handoff packets and no longer hosts application implementations.
decisions.critiqueSummary: The repo drifted into building products in-repo, which contradicted the owner's clarified intent for IsIdeas.
analytical: Handoff-ready ideas leave active frontier work and application code is quarantined out of repo scope.

decisions.title: Codex-led governance reset
decisions.summary: Retire the old ChatGPT-led workshop model and replace it with a Codex-led R&D command system.
decisions.critiqueSummary: The old model kept Codex too weak to own the control tower and slowed coherent system design.
analytical: A single accountable product lead is better than mixed judgment lanes for this repo's current maturity.

decisions.title: Postgres runtime activation must leave an explicit trace
decisions.summary: Local Postgres activation is allowed, but the storage transition must resolve F-001 explicitly rather than silently deleting it.
decisions.critiqueSummary: Addresses Claude's critical finding that the original Postgres adapter erased an integrity alarm with no record.
analytical: Operational storage backends may change by environment, but the transition itself must stay visible in the repo's integrity history.

decisions.title: Frontier ideas require independent critique artifacts
decisions.summary: A frontier idea cannot rely only on Codex-authored judgment. It must point at an independent critique artifact or an explicit owner override.
decisions.critiqueSummary: This breaks the self-confirming proposer-recorder-critic loop identified in the adversarial review.
analytical: The owner should not be dragged into routine approvals, so external critique artifacts become the default breaker of circular self-ratification.

decisions.title: Retire the undefined candidate stage
decisions.summary: The active idea set uses dossier-grade artifacts, so the undefined `candidate` label is retired in favor of the documented lifecycle stages.
decisions.critiqueSummary: This addresses the review finding that the repo was simultaneously documenting one lifecycle vocabulary while storing another.
analytical: Active ideas with developed DOSSIER.md files are better described as dossier-stage workspaces than as undefined candidates.

decisions.title: Treat kr as a real but still underdefined dependency
decisions.summary: The repo may rely on kr conceptually, but any idea blocked on a stable kr machine contract must say so explicitly instead of masking the dependency.
decisions.critiqueSummary: Addresses the review finding that kr was being treated as settled infrastructure while remaining under-specified for build-grade integration.
analytical: Boundary docs are useful but insufficient; build readiness requires explicit contract honesty.

decisions.title: Narrow the frontier after dual independent critique
decisions.summary: Keep Fixed-Text Preservation System as the sole frontier/deep-focus idea and demote Curriculum Architect to challenge/visible until it survives a modelability and authority-boundary test.
decisions.critiqueSummary: The independent critiques returned `mixed` for Fixed-Text Preservation System and `weakens` for Curriculum Architect, so deep attention should narrow rather than stay split.
analytical: The system has now produced enough independent pressure to make a real frontier decision instead of keeping both ideas deep by inertia.

bottlenecks.title: Exact-text preservation over years
bottlenecks.uncertaintyNote: Working study hypothesis based on preparation goals and memorization-risk analysis; still needs explicit source-backed grounding.
bottlenecks.whyItMatters: Losing memorized fixed text wastes years of compounding effort and attacks the foundations-first proving ground directly.
bottlenecks.nextMove: Pressure-test the preservation thesis against acquisition, review honesty, and Qur'an-first constraints.

bottlenecks.title: Curriculum sequencing and prerequisite discipline
bottlenecks.uncertaintyNote: This sequencing claim is plausible but not yet backed here by explicit scholarly or pedagogical sourcing.
bottlenecks.whyItMatters: A serious student can waste years reading in the wrong order or without noticing dependency gaps.
bottlenecks.nextMove: Map the earliest-stage sciences and the shift from basics to advanced pathways without false authority.

bottlenecks.title: Question capture and gap closure
bottlenecks.uncertaintyNote: Uncertainty preservation is treated as strategically important, but the repo still needs source-backed study-process grounding.
bottlenecks.whyItMatters: Unresolved confusion compounds silently and becomes fake understanding if not tracked and revisited.
bottlenecks.nextMove: Design a loop that preserves uncertainty and resolution lineage instead of rewarding premature closure.

bottlenecks.title: Lecture and teacher interaction continuity
bottlenecks.uncertaintyNote: This bottleneck is inferred from likely future workflows and remains open to revision after real lecture-system research.
bottlenecks.whyItMatters: Formal study and private study both lose value when attendance, notes, transcripts, and follow-up questions drift apart.
bottlenecks.nextMove: Scout a lecture-ingestion and follow-up system without blurring into generic note-taking.

bottlenecks.title: Global study operations awareness
bottlenecks.uncertaintyNote: This is a meta-level operational hypothesis and should not outrank direct study leverage without stronger evidence.
bottlenecks.whyItMatters: A canonical hub needs a cockpit for what is fragile, stale, active, and strategically under-built.
bottlenecks.nextMove: Keep this as a meta-layer, not a distraction from direct study leverage.

ideas.name: Fixed-Text Preservation System
ideas.thesis: Preserve already-memorized exact text with an honest review loop instead of building a generic memorization app.
ideas.nextMove: Hand off the packet elsewhere and preserve the factory lessons here.
ideas.mainRisk: Scope drift could pull this factory back into hosting product implementation.
ideas.krRelationship: Consumes canonical fixed text from kr but does not own source preservation.
ideas.krDependencyNote: The idea is packeted for external build work; the provisional Qur'an reference contract stays documented here but not implemented here.

ideas.name: Curriculum Architect
ideas.thesis: Turn long-horizon study sequencing into an explicit, revisable system instead of scattered instincts.
ideas.nextMove: Run a concrete modelability test and define the authority-boundary mechanism before it can re-enter deep attention.
ideas.mainRisk: The idea may be too abstract and too authority-sensitive to justify current deep-focus investment.
ideas.krRelationship: Depends on topics, sources, and excerpt relationships from kr while owning study behavior above them.
ideas.krDependencyNote: Real build work is blocked until kr defines a deeper pedagogical ontology than currently exists.

ideas.name: Question & Confusion Ledger
ideas.thesis: Track confusion as a first-class study object so uncertainty is preserved, revisited, and eventually resolved.
ideas.nextMove: Model uncertainty states and resolution lineage before deciding whether this deserves renewed frontier attention.
ideas.mainRisk: A neat ledger can create the illusion that a question has been resolved when it has only been logged.
ideas.krRelationship: Links back to excerpts, topics, and sources in kr instead of duplicating them.
ideas.krDependencyNote: Question workflow design can proceed now, but deep integration depends on kr entity IDs and excerpt linkage contracts.

ideas.name: Lecture Ingestion Ledger
ideas.thesis: Capture attended lessons, transcripts, linked notes, and follow-up actions without collapsing into generic note apps.
ideas.nextMove: Decide whether this is a standalone idea or a module inside the future canonical hub.
ideas.mainRisk: This can easily devolve into a nicer notebook with no strategic leverage.
ideas.krRelationship: May link lecture artifacts into kr later, but should begin as workflow intelligence above the substrate.
ideas.krDependencyNote: Early workflow design can proceed without a hard kr contract, though later integration may enrich lecture artifacts with source links.

submissions.title: Attendance and lesson history tracker
submissions.context: Need continuity on what lectures I attended, what was covered, and how that links back to the rest of the knowledge environment.
submissions.whyNow: This is one of the most obvious examples of the total-environment thesis.
submissions.desiredOutcome: Challenge whether this deserves its own candidate or belongs under a bigger lecture-ingestion system.
submissions.reviewNote: Likely belongs under Lecture Ingestion Ledger unless a deeper bottleneck appears.

tools.name: WSL2 runtime clone
tools.note: Preferred unattended host so long runs stay isolated from the interactive Windows checkout.

tools.name: Codex CLI
tools.note: Primary writing and integration lane for the control tower and future handoff packets.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.note: Useful as an independent verification or exploration lane when cross-provider disagreement matters.

tools.name: Gemini CLI
tools.note: Useful as a local multi-provider research or verification lane without requiring a separate API key.

tools.name: Paperclip
tools.note: Reference for multi-agent orchestration and UI, but not yet trusted as the canonical core.

tools.name: pg-boss
tools.note: Candidate durable queue for the next runtime step after the current state-model reconciliation.

researchArtifacts.title: Autonomous system architecture survey
researchArtifacts.focus: Control plane options, WSL runtime, durable queues, and local-first orchestration.
researchArtifacts.note: Paperclip, OpenAI Agents SDK, and Temporal have already been sampled.

researchArtifacts.title: Foundations-first bottleneck map
researchArtifacts.focus: Qur'an, Arabic, memorization, question capture, and private study continuity.
researchArtifacts.note: This remains a working analytical map and still needs explicit source-backed grounding.

integrityFlags.title: Storage backend transition must be explicitly recorded
integrityFlags.detail: The tracked seed snapshot begins in bootstrap file-store mode. If a local runtime activates Postgres, the transition must resolve this flag explicitly rather than silently deleting it.

integrityFlags.title: Seed portfolio still reflects workshop-era assumptions
integrityFlags.detail: Factory scope drift has been corrected, but the next frontier still needs deliberate selection and the bottleneck map still needs renewed pressure.

loops.name: Intake triage
loops.purpose: Accept owner submissions, dedupe them, and queue them for critique without auto-promoting them.
loops.failureImpact: Owner submissions can queue silently and intake confidence decays.
loops.lastOutcome: Bootstrap placeholder only; no verified post-reset intake run recorded yet.

loops.name: Bottleneck map refresh
loops.purpose: Keep coverage focused on deep recurring losses instead of drifting into feature excitement.
loops.failureImpact: The bottleneck map can go stale and keep selecting the wrong frontier ideas.
loops.lastOutcome: No dedicated bottleneck refresh run has been recorded since the Codex-led reset.

loops.name: Tool scouting
loops.purpose: Continuously compare available agent, search, and workflow tools before custom implementation expands.
loops.failureImpact: Tool scouting can become decorative, causing the repo to reinvent known solutions.
loops.lastOutcome: Initial survey completed, but no recurring scouting accountability exists yet.

loops.name: Red-team critique
loops.purpose: Attack the strongest candidates for false authority, genericness, and weak substrate assumptions.
loops.failureImpact: Frontier ideas can self-confirm if critique stops arriving.
loops.lastOutcome: External adversarial review exists, but no recurring independent critique cadence has been logged yet.

loops.name: Morning report
loops.purpose: Generate one compact report of focus, risk, integrity, and queue state for the next session.
loops.failureImpact: The repo can appear active while runtime accountability silently drifts.

summary: Bootstrap file-store runtime prepared for local use.
Existing idea seeds retained only as contestable candidates.
